dataset: CCAMTest (CCAM - Classification Commune des Actes Médicaux)

pi_items.verified_value: Official MedinTux v2.16.012 installer bundled SQL dump at ~/.wine/drive_c/MedinTux-2.16/Programmes/set_bases/bin/SqlCreateTable/Dump_CCAMTest.sql
pi_items.source: setup_medintux.sh lines 244-248 load Dump_CCAMTest.sql from official installer; confirmed in env_creation_notes/medintux_env/notes.md line 83

CCAM is the official French Classification Commune des Actes Médicaux (official medical procedure codes), not synthetic. Wikipedia and multiple French health authority sources confirm CCAM is the real French medical procedure classification used since 2005

Multiple sources confirm CCAM nomenclature contains approximately 7,500 different medical procedure codes. Web search results from Wikipedia, Health Data Hub (documentation-snds.health-data-hub.fr), and CCAM documentation confirm this count
Task description mentions codes like AAFA001, and web search results confirm CCAM structure is 4 letters followed by 3 digits
install_medintux.sh line 47-50 downloads medintux-2.16.012.exe; confirmed in env_creation_notes notes.md line 5
install_medintux.sh line 48; confirmed in env_creation_notes/medintux_env/notes.md line 10

privileged_info_summary: The CCAMTest database contains real CCAM (Classification Commune des Actes Médicaux) codes - the official French medical procedure classification system introduced in 2005. The data is not synthetic but sourced from the official MedinTux v2.16.012 (2014-07-11) installer bundled SQL dump. CCAM comprises approximately 7,500+ medical procedure codes using a semi-structured format of 4 letters followed by 3 digits (e.g., AAFA001), organized hierarchically by anatomical location and procedure type. The database is configured to be accessible via MySQL on localhost (root user, no password) in the CCAMTest database.
